data.body:
[G3 child-coverage audit — parent 72d5 (Migrate the LLM gates onto the workflow engine) vs child B6 (Remove throwaway sample workflows; confirm two-real-workflow end state)] Clean — no findings.

4-bucket audit of parent acceptance/success criteria against this child, with whole-roster absence cross-check:
- Parent AC5 (throwaway samples removed — examples/code_review.yaml + templates.py per Joe's selection; forwarding shims/public API preserved for anything still referenced; epic A's demo workflow retained as the visual-editing sample): FULLY covered by this child. B6 AC1 deletes code_review.yaml + templates.py and keeps `rebar workflow new` working via a preserved shim/documented replacement; B6 AC2 retains examples/review_skeleton.yaml and asserts grep-clean (no orphaned references).
- Parent AC6 (end state: exactly two real LLM workflows + the one retained sample; `make lint` + full test suite pass): FULLY covered by this child. B6 AC3 confirms the exact two-real-workflow + one-sample inventory; B6 AC4 requires lint + full suite green.
- Parent success criterion, "only real workflows remain (samples removed)" half: FULLY covered by this child (AC1–AC3). The "both gates run on the ONE engine substrate, prompts in the engine prompt library, validated by offline planned-trace parity" half is structural/not-this-child — covered by B2 (6b06, plan-review workflow) + B3 (dc58, completion-verifier workflow) + B4 (101a, parity) + B5 (f12d, cutover) + B-DM (5506, prompt/criteria library).
- Parent AC1 (plan-review as engine workflow + production batch-runner from sizing.py): structural/not-this-child — fully covered by B2 (6b06) + B1 (723c).
- Parent AC2 (verify_completion as engine workflow, existing step kinds): structural/not-this-child — fully covered by B3 (dc58).
- Parent AC3 (planned-trace parity harness, offline FakeRunner, CI): structural/not-this-child — fully covered by B4 (101a).
- Parent AC4 (bespoke path cut over behind the gate once parity holds; close/claim gates keep working, signed verdicts/attestations intact): structural/not-this-child — fully covered by B5 (f12d) + 0408.

Every parent acceptance/success criterion is either fully covered by this child (the samples-removal + two-real-workflow end-state criteria AC5, AC6, and the "only real workflows remain" half of the SC) or fully covered by a named sibling already in the roster. No uncovered parent criterion is attributable to this pairing. Note: B6's depends-on-B5 line is consistent with parent AC4/AC5 ordering (samples are truly unused only after cutover). No findings.
